breaking_news.title: Iran-Linked Handala Hackers Breach FBI Chief Kash Patel’s Gmail
breaking_news.summary:
Iran-linked Handala hackers successfully breached the personal Gmail account of FBI Chief Kash Patel, leaking photos and documents to the public. Officials confirmed that while personal data was exposed, no classified government information was compromised during the intrusion.

This incident highlights the targeting of high-value government officials by state-sponsored actors, raising concerns about personal security for top law enforcement leadership. The breach underscores the persistent threat of espionage campaigns against key figures in national security agencies.

breaking_news.title: TeamPCP Compromises Trivy, LiteLLM, and Telnyx in Massive Supply Chain Attack
breaking_news.summary:
The threat actor TeamPCP executed a sophisticated supply chain attack by compromising the Trivy, LiteLLM, and Telnyx packages on PyPI and GitHub. Malicious versions were pushed to steal cloud credentials, SSH keys, and Kubernetes secrets from thousands of developer environments.

CISA has added the associated vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, warning of widespread impact on SaaS environments and AI workflows. This campaign demonstrates the escalating risk to open-source software ecosystems and the potential for cascading infrastructure compromises.

breaking_news.title: European Commission Confirms Data Theft from Cloud Infrastructure
breaking_news.summary:
The European Commission confirmed a cyberattack on its cloud infrastructure hosting Europa.eu websites, resulting in the theft of over 350GB of data. The incident involved compromised Amazon Web Services accounts, though the Commission stated internal networks remained unaffected.

EU entities are being notified of potential data exposure as the investigation continues to determine the full scope of the breach. This event represents a significant security incident for a major governing body, impacting trust in cloud infrastructure security.

breaking_news.title: China-Linked Red Menshen APT Deploys Stealthy Implants in Telecom Networks
breaking_news.summary:
The China-linked Red Menshen APT group has been deploying stealthy BPFDoor implants within telecom networks across the Middle East and Asia. These implants act as digital sleeper cells, allowing long-term espionage on government communications and critical infrastructure.

Researchers describe the implants as extremely difficult to detect, posing a severe risk to national security and population-wide communications. The strategic positioning within telecom backbones enables attackers to monitor sensitive data flows without triggering standard alerts.

breaking_news.title: Attackers Exploit Critical Langflow RCE Within Hours of Disclosure
breaking_news.summary:
Attackers actively exploited a critical Remote Code Execution vulnerability in the Langflow AI framework within 20 hours of its disclosure. CISA has flagged CVE-2026-33017 for urgent remediation as threat actors weaponized the flaw to hijack AI workflows and exfiltrate environment variables.

The rapid exploitation highlights the vulnerability of open-source AI tools, with honeypot nodes observing multiple attack attempts across cloud providers. Organizations using Langflow must immediately patch or isolate instances to prevent unauthorized code execution and data theft.

breaking_news.title: CISA and BSI Warn of Critical PTC Windchill Flaw with No Patch
breaking_news.summary:
CISA and the German BSI issued warnings about a critical Remote Code Execution flaw in PTC Windchill and FlexPLM software tracked as CVE-2026-4681. German police physically mobilized to warn organizations about the imminent risk, as no patches are currently available.

The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code through deserialization of untrusted data, posing a severe threat to product lifecycle management systems. Companies relying on PTC software are urged to apply immediate mitigations while awaiting vendor updates.

breaking_news.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Linked to Operation Triangulation Campaign
breaking_news.summary:
Researchers discovered that the Coruna iOS exploit kit reuses updated kernel exploit code from the 2023 Operation Triangulation campaign. This sophisticated tool targets iPhones running iOS versions 13.0 through 17.2.1 with multiple exploit chains and zero-click capabilities.

The link suggests a possible connection to previous state-sponsored espionage efforts, indicating an evolution in mobile targeting tactics. Apple users on older iOS versions are urged to update immediately to protect against these advanced exploitation frameworks.

breaking_news.title: Lapsus$ Claims Breach of Pharma Giant AstraZeneca
breaking_news.summary:
The cybercrime group Lapsus$ claimed responsibility for hacking AstraZeneca, stealing approximately 3GB of sensitive data including credentials and internal code. The group advertised the breach on dark web forums, though the pharmaceutical company has not yet officially confirmed the incident.

Stolen data could facilitate further attacks against the healthcare sector, including phishing campaigns and system mapping. This incident highlights the ongoing threat of ransomware and extortion groups targeting critical healthcare infrastructure.
